item.title: Supreme Court clears path for Alabama to redraw congressional map
item.description: The Supreme Court set aside lower court decisions that had blocked the state from using a congressional map drawn by Republicans in 2023 that contained one majority-Black district.

item.title: Cambridge gunman was shooting passing cars at random, witnesses say
item.description: There is a massive police response on Memorial Drive in Cambridge Monday afternoon after witnesses say a man with a long gun opened fire on passing cars.

item.title: Americans exposed to hantavirus return to quarantine in U.S.
item.description: The Americans who were exposed to hantavirus aboard a cruise ship are now quarantining back in the U.S. CBS News chief medical correspondent Dr. Jon LaPook joins to explain what happens next and the current risk of spread.

item.title: Netanyahu blames foreign bots on social media for decline of U.S. support for Israel
item.description: Israel has seen its support in the U.S. deteriorate since the start of the war in Gaza. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett asked Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu about why that might be. Then, former CIA officer and Marine Corps veteran Elliot Ackerman joins to unpack Netanyahu's answer.

item.title: War not over as Iran still has nuclear materials, Netanyahu says
item.description: CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu about where the war with Iran stands and what it would take to end the conflict. Former Trump national security adviser H. R. McMaster joins with analysis of Netanyahu's response.

item.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
item.description: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

item.title: Netanyahu says "nobody had perfect foresight" on Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett asked Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu about The New York Times reporting that, in pre-war conversations with President Trump, Netanyahu and his team asserted that Iran would not choke off the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes joins with Mr. Trump's response.

item.title: CDC leader says hantavirus not "a five-alarm fire bell"
item.description: Jay Bhattacharya, the acting director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, told CBS News that the hantavirus outbreak should be treated differently from COVID.

item.title: Iran vows to fight on as Trump says ceasefire is "on life support"
item.description: President Trump is expected to encourage China to pressure Iran into making a deal to end the war when he visits Beijing later this week and meets with President Xi Jinping.

item.title: Acting CDC director says hantavirus is "very different than COVID," defends agency's response
item.description: Acting CDC Director Jay Bhattacharya talks to "CBS Evening News" anchor Tony Dokoupil about the U.S. response to the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, saying the disease is "very different than COVID. And we should treat it differently than COVID."

item.title: The stakes of Trump's 2-day summit with Xi Jinping
item.description: President Trump will head to Beijing on Tuesday for a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Henrietta Levin, senior fellow at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, joins CBS News with analysis.

item.title: Stocks remain strong despite Iran war uncertainty
item.description: Despite the ongoing war in Iran and high gas prices, stocks started trading higher on Monday. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq both hit record highs, bolstered by the tech sector. Yahoo Finance senior reporter Brooke DiPalma joins with analysis.

item.title: Infectious disease specialist commends U.S. hantavirus response: "Very appropriate"
item.description: A flight carrying 18 Americans who were aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship landed in the U.S. on Monday. Dr. Peter Chin-Hong, infectious disease specialist at the University of California, San Francisco, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Virginia Democrats ask Supreme Court to reinstate blocked congressional map
item.description: Virginia Democrats asked the Supreme Court to restore its congressional map that aimed to give Democrats an edge in the midterms, days after it was blocked​ by the state's highest court.

item.title: Elon Musk and other CEOs invited to be in Trump's delegation to China
item.description: Elon Musk, Tim Cook and other high-powered business leaders have been invited to be a part of the U.S. delegation traveling to China this week.

item.title: Supreme Court continues to temporarily maintain mail access to abortion pill
item.description: Justice Samuel Alito extended an administrative stay that maintained access to mifepristone through the mail.

item.title: White House ballroom funds hang over GOP push to fund ICE
item.description: The Senate is returning to Washington to resume work on funding immigration agencies with a package that includes $1 billion for the renovation of the White House East Wing.

item.title: Texas sues Netflix for allegedly spying on kids and consumers
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has sued Netflix for allegedly spying on kids and consumers by illegally collecting data without users' knowledge. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: Why hantavirus is not like COVID, according to infectious disease experts
item.description: Infectious disease experts have sought to reassure people that the hantavirus cruise ship outbreak poses very low risks to the wider public.

item.title: Man accused of starting Palisades Fire admired Luigi Mangione, prosecutors say
item.description: The man accused of starting last year's catastrophic Palisades Fire in Los Angeles appeared in court Monday. Prosecutors said the suspect admired Luigi Mangione, who is accused of murdering UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. CBS News' Carter Evans has more.

item.title: Mediator Pakistan allowed Iran to park military aircraft on its airfields
item.description: The effort potentially shielded Iranian aircraft from American airstrikes, according to U.S. officials with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Cole Allen's lawyers seek to disqualify D.C. attorneys from correspondents' dinner shooting case
item.description: The lawyers for the man accused of attacking the White House Correspondents' Dinner are seeking to disqualify top prosecutor Jeanine Pirro, acting Attorney General Todd Blanche and other U.S. attorneys in the Washington, D.C. office from the case. CBS News' Jake Rosen has more.

item.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire agreement is on "massive life support"
item.description: President Trump told reporters in the Oval Office on Monday the U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement is on "massive life support." CBS News' Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: Cartel figure detained as officials recover drugs, guns and 7 tigers
item.description: Authorities said they seized unidentified narcotics, cash, 10 guns, 11 vehicles, six motorcycles — and seven tigers.

item.title: Years after sex abuse scandal, a young gymnast says "it happened again"
item.description: Sean Gardner, a gymnastics coach who trained elite young girls, will be in federal court in Mississippi on Monday facing 12 felony counts of sexual exploitation of children.

item.title: Why Americans from the hantavirus-stricken ship were taken to Omaha
item.description: Most of the Americans who were on a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak were taken to specialized facilities at the University of Nebraska Medical Center.

item.title: Trump approves plan to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Marty Makary has served as Food and Drug Administration commissioner since March 2025.

item.title: Trump says he aims to suspend gas tax "for a period of time"
item.description: President Trump made the comments in a phone interview with CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes.

item.title: Principal who tackled gunman says he didn't initially know he was shot
item.description: Kirk Moore, the principal at Pauls Valley High School in Oklahoma, exclusively told CBS News how he acted on "just instinct" when tackling a school shooter.

item.title: U.S. plans evacuation for Americans on cruise ship in hantavirus outbreak
item.description: The MV Hondius is currently traveling to the Canary Islands, where the 147 people on board will be methodically off-boarded and flown home.
